summary: Attempted to match three library functions: fn_800263B4 in lbaudio_ax.c, fn_80022608 in lbrefract.c, and lbBgFlash_80021C48 in lbbgflash.c. The PR also flipped lbrefract.c and lbaudio_ax.c to Matching in configure.py, adjusted a lbbgflash.h prototype, and changed ftCo_8009DB50 in ftdynamics.c. It was closed unmerged because the batched worktree commits had build issues; the diff excerpt shows unresolved conflict markers in lbbgflash.c, so the matching claims should not be treated as accepted.
what_changed: Added an implementation of fn_800263B4 in src/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax.c: null-checks a pointer and calls HSD_ObjFree(&lbl_80433710, p).

Added lbRefract_800225D4 and fn_80022608 in src/melee/lb/lbrefract.c. fn_80022608 dispatches through hsdDObj.disp(dobj, vmtx, pmtx, rendermode), then resets GX indirect/TEV state via GXSetTevDirect(0), GXSetNumIndStages(0), and HSD_StateInvalidate(-1).
Added baselib/dobj.h and baselib/state.h includes in lbrefract.c and declared extern HSD_DObjInfo hsdDObj.
Attempted to implement lbBgFlash_80021C48 in src/melee/lb/lbbgflash.c by deriving a ColorOverlay pointer from lbl_804D63E0->x2C + 4 and passing it with lbl_804D63DC and two u32 args to lb_800144C8.
Introduced a local BgFlashGlobal struct with char pad[0x2C] and void* x2C plus externs for lbl_804D63E0 and lbl_804D63DC in lbbgflash.c.
Changed the lbbgflash.h prototype for lbBgFlash_800205F0 from void lbBgFlash_800205F0(void) to void lbBgFlash_800205F0(int).

Changed configure.py so lbrefract.c and lbaudio_ax.c were marked Matching instead of NonMatching; lbbgflash.c remained NonMatching in the shown excerpt despite the PR title claiming a lbbgflash match.

Refactored ftCo_8009DB50 in ftdynamics.c by replacing PAD_STACK and an inner scoped loop variable with ssize_t i and j declarations, caching bone_id in an int, and indexing hat->hat_dynamics[4]->ftDynamicBones->array[i] directly.
smart_moves: For fn_800263B4, the implementation kept the simple object-freeing pattern explicit: preserve the null guard and call HSD_ObjFree with the known object heap/list symbol lbl_80433710.
For fn_80022608, the added code pairs the DObj display callback with explicit GX/HSD state cleanup, a reusable pattern for display wrappers that temporarily alter render state.
The lbrefract work included the headers needed to express real HSD_DObj and state APIs instead of relying only on placeholders.

The lbbgflash attempt modeled an unknown global with a minimal padded struct to access a known field offset, which can be a useful interim matching tactic when a full global struct is not yet recovered.
decomp_lessons: Do not batch or submit generated matching work with unresolved merge conflict markers; the lbbgflash.c diff contains <<<<<<<, =======, and >>>>>>> markers and the PR was closed for build issues.
Only flip objects to Matching in configure.py after the entire object builds and verifies; this PR marked lbrefract.c and lbaudio_ax.c Matching but was still closed for source/build problems.
When matching wrappers around HSD/GX display code, include the correct baselib headers and express calls through known dispatch tables like hsdDObj.disp where evidence supports it.
Offset-based temporary structs such as BgFlashGlobal with pad fields can unblock matching, but should be treated as provisional until the real global type is understood and headerized.
Header prototype fixes can be part of matching work; here lbBgFlash_800205F0 was changed to take an int, but such signature changes must be validated across call sites.
Non-target file edits, like the ftdynamics.c refactor, increase risk in a small matching PR unless they are necessary for the match or build.
naming_conventions: Library functions use subsystem prefixes such as lbAudioAx_, lbRefract_, and lbBgFlash_ followed by the original address, e.g. lbBgFlash_80021C48.
Unidentified local/static functions retain fn_XXXXXXXX address names, e.g. fn_800263B4 and fn_80022608.
Unknown globals retain lbl_XXXXXXXX names, e.g. lbl_80433710, lbl_804D63E0, lbl_804D63DC, and lbl_804D63D8.
Temporary recovered structs may be named after the subsystem/global role, as with BgFlashGlobal, but the evidence here is limited to a single accessed offset.
Fighter common functions use ftCo_ address names, e.g. ftCo_8009DB50.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use an explicit null check before HSD_ObjFree to match simple allocator/free helper control flow.
Introduce a temporary local pointer variable before HSD_ObjFree when needed to reproduce register allocation or code shape.
Call through hsdDObj.disp rather than a direct display function when matching DObj render dispatch behavior.
Reset render state after drawing with GXSetTevDirect(0), GXSetNumIndStages(0), and HSD_StateInvalidate(-1), matching a common post-render cleanup sequence.
Use padded structs and byte-pointer arithmetic to represent known offsets into unresolved globals, e.g. casting lbl_804D63E0->x2C plus 4 to ColorOverlay*.

Loop reshaping in ftdynamics.c replaced a descriptor pointer with repeated indexed accesses and cached bone_id, likely aiming to alter generated code shape, but the PR evidence does not show whether this was required for a match.

review_feedback.feedback: PR was closed because the worktree commits had build issues that needed to be fixed at the source before batching.
lbbgflash.c contains unresolved conflict markers, directly explaining or contributing to the build issues.

follow_up_queries: Find the later PR or commit that successfully matched fn_800263B4 in lbaudio_ax.c after this closed batch.
Find the accepted implementation of fn_80022608 and lbRefract_800225D4 to confirm the HSD/GX state cleanup pattern.
Search for the final resolved lbBgFlash_80021C48 implementation and whether BgFlashGlobal was replaced by a real global struct.
Check whether lbBgFlash_800205F0(int) became the accepted prototype across all call sites.
Investigate why ftdynamics.c was modified in this batch and whether those changes were retained elsewhere.
